_comment: CNA campaign victory geography (rule 64.7). Hex labels transcribed from docs/rules/90 'SUMMARY OF IMPORTANT LOCATIONS', cross-validated against scenario-setup rules 60/61/62 and the engine/OOB constants. VP values are the rule-64.73 Geographic Occupation Point table. Consumed by game.campaign_victory.
_supply_sources_note: The two harbours rules 64.71 and 64.72 name: a Supply Dump must be feedable from one of them 'in any way' for a combat unit's truck-MP line of supply to it to count. Consumed by game.campaign_victory.CampaignVictory.supply_sources; a null 'hex' is a source this map does not carry and is absent from the trace. Each source also states whether rule 56.15 can shut it -- see _capturable_note.
_capturable_note: WHICH SOURCE RULE 56.15 CAN SHUT, and it is a rulebook fact per source, not a dial. 56.15: 'a convoy scheduled to arrive at a port that is captured by the Commonwealth is cancelled. It never sails.' TOBRUK is an on-map PORT the Commonwealth can take, so the gate is live (capturable true). TRIPOLI is an off-map BOX (8.81) and 56.15 is NOT switched off for it -- its ANTECEDENT IS UNSATISFIABLE: 8.82 says 'No Commonwealth land or sea unit may ever enter any of the boxes', so a Commonwealth capture of Tripoli is impossible and the convoy is never cancelled (capturable false). This key exists because A2802 is a PROXY (see _tripoli_proxy_flag) and a proxy must not inherit a rule the thing it proxies is exempt from: A2802 is an ordinary desert hex the Commonwealth may walk onto, and with the gate applied to it, Control.ALLIED on Tobruk AND A2802 took the campaign board from 13 fed dumps to 0 and cut all 177 Axis combat units out of the 60-MP trace -- a rule-64.72 Commonwealth auto-win from Game-Turn 35, bought with one unit on one far-west hex, with the whole Panzerarmee alive and stocked. The book contains no such win. The Commonwealth can still shut the road by STANDING on it, under 10.29 (a Truck Convoy may not enter an unnegated enemy ZOC): measured on the same board with Tobruk captured, ONE Commonwealth battalion on A2802 leaves all 13 dumps fed (10.11 exerts no ZOC below 2 Stacking Points) and TWO take the Axis to 0. That block is the book's and is not what the capture gate did -- a ZOC needs a force standing there and the Axis can answer it (10.26 negation, or drive it off), where capture needs no force at all and no Axis action lifts it.
_tripoli_note: THE TRIPOLI HOLE IS CLOSED (2026-07-16). Tripoli is off-map (8.81: the Tripoli/Tunisia boxes sit on the western edge of Map A; 8.88 makes them Supply Dumps of unlimited capacity; 22.31 names the Tripoli Box). Its ON-MAP GATEWAY IS HEX A2802, and that is the rulebook's own number, not a reading: 8.85 says 'For a unit to be moved off the game map towards Tripolitania it must start that Operations Stage in hex A2802', and A2802 is one step from Nofilia (A2703) along the coast road. This key was null because data/terrain_A.json colour-samples A2802 as SEA -- the Gulf of Sirte coastline is sampled roughly one hex too far inland. That is now overridden AT THE MAP LAYER from the rulebook (game.cna_map._RULEBOOK_LAND, citing 8.85), which is the same override cna_map._load_edges already applies from the roads data ('a road runs on land') and game.scenario._connect_pieces applies from the OOB ('a hex where a land unit stands is land'). A2802 is adjacent to A2801/A2702/A2703, all already land, so the gateway joins the mainland with no bridging.
_tripoli_proxy_flag: PROXY, FLAGGED: hex A2802 stands for the off-map Tripoli/Tunisia boxes as a 64.71/64.72 supply SOURCE. The book makes Tripoli an off-map box of unlimited capacity (8.88) and names A2802 as the road hex units enter the map through (8.85); this engine has no off-map box, so the source is anchored at the gateway hex. WHAT THE PROXY DOES *NOT* CARRY, stated here because the flag used to disclose the geometry and stay silent on the behaviour, and the behaviour was the defect: anchoring an off-map source on a capturable ON-MAP hex made an UNCAPTURABLE source capturable, which is a Commonwealth automatic win rule 8.82 forbids. That is now closed at the source-kind ('capturable': false -- see _capturable_note), not in the flag. The remaining proxy gaps all UNDERSTATE the Axis road home rather than overstate it, so each can only make 64.72 fire MORE readily than the book, never less: A2803/A2804 (named in 8.85's own 'e.g.') and A1816/El Agheila (61.43C names it a road hex) remain sea, so Tripoli enters through a ONE-hex gateway where the book gives it a road, and the gateway is CLEAR rather than road because roads are edges in this pipeline (data/roads_A.json) and seeding one would invent a road net the extraction does not carry. THE ONE-HEX GATEWAY HAS TEETH AND THEY ARE MEASURED: two Commonwealth combat units standing ON A2802 put its three existing neighbours in ZOC and take the Axis to 0 fed dumps (10.29). That block is faithful -- it is a force holding a road, which the Axis can negate (10.26) or drive off, not a capture -- but the book gives Tripolitania a road FRONTAGE (8.85's 2802/2803/2804, plus El Agheila A1816 per 61.43C) where this map gives it a single hex, so the chokepoint is ours and the map job is the fix.
